skill.name: competitor-alternatives
skill.description: Create competitor comparison and alternative pages for SEO, sales enablement, and buyer education.
skill.summary: Build fair, structured competitor alternative pages and comparison content.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found Markdown URL examples, fenced content examples, and plain text templates. No executable scripts, shell calls, network calls, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Command Matches
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The flagged backticks appear in Markdown URL patterns, fenced Markdown, YAML, and JSON examples, not in executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown syntax and content examples. There is no interpreter context, subprocess API, or executable script around these matches.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives. The flagged locations contain metadata, comparison templates, pricing text, and customer persona text, not cryptographic algorithms or hash usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain instructional copy and placeholders. I found no MD5, SHA1, cipher configuration, password hashing, or security-sensitive cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding is a false positive. The line advises avoiding duplicated competitor research and does not request host, network, environment, or account discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a content architecture principle. No commands, file enumeration, environment inspection, or system discovery behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Competitive SEO pages can become shallow, inaccurate, or hard to maintain. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured process for fair comparison pages.
actual_capabilities: Defines four competitor page formats, including alternative, alternatives, and versus pages.
Provides page structures for comparison pages, index pages, and competitor hubs.
Outlines centralized competitor data fields for reusable product research.
Supplies section templates for summaries, features, pricing, support, migration, and social proof.
Explains research inputs from pricing, reviews, product testing, and customer feedback.
Recommends SEO targeting, internal linking, and FAQ schema considerations.
limitations: Does not perform live competitor research without separate browsing or data access.
Does not verify pricing, features, or customer quotes automatically.
Does not generate final designs, screenshots, or page implementation code.
Requires product positioning and competitor inputs for accurate output.

use_cases.title: Launch Alternative Pages
use_cases.description: Create focused pages for buyers searching for an alternative to a known competitor.

use_cases.title: Support Sales Comparisons
use_cases.description: Build fair comparison narratives that explain strengths, tradeoffs, pricing, and migration paths.

use_cases.title: Scale Competitive SEO
use_cases.description: Plan reusable competitor data and hub pages for a larger comparison content program.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Alternative Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline an alternative page for [Competitor]. My product is [Product], and our main differentiator is [Differentiator].

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Versus Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a [Product] versus [Competitor] page structure. Include honest strengths, limitations, pricing notes, and buyer-fit guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Build Competitor Data Inputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to define a centralized competitor profile for [Competitor]. Include positioning, pricing, features, strengths, weaknesses, reviews, and migration notes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Comparison Content Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a competitor comparison page set for these competitors: [List]. Prioritize pages by intent, SEO value, sales value, and maintenance effort.

output_examples.input: Create a Notion alternative page outline for a project management product.
output: A search-intent summary for people considering a switch from Notion.
A page outline with positioning, feature comparison, migration guidance, proof, and calls to action.
Notes on where Notion is strong and where the alternative may fit better.

output_examples.input: Plan a hub for all comparison pages.
output: A recommended alternatives index, versus index, and cross-linking structure.
Suggested page groups by competitor, format, and search intent.
Maintenance guidance for updating pricing and feature claims.

output_examples.input: Create a reusable competitor research profile.
output: A structured competitor profile covering positioning, pricing, features, strengths, weaknesses, and migration notes.
Research prompts for reviews, customer feedback, documentation, and recent product changes.

best_practices: Use current, verifiable competitor data before publishing claims.
Acknowledge competitor strengths and your own limitations clearly.
Keep competitor data centralized so updates remain consistent across pages.
anti_patterns: Publishing comparison pages based only on feature checklists.
Misrepresenting competitor pricing, features, support, or customer fit.
Creating many pages without a maintenance plan for changing claims.

faq.question: What types of pages does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It supports singular alternative pages, plural alternatives pages, direct versus pages, competitor-versus-competitor pages, and index pages.

faq.question: Can it replace competitor research?
faq.answer: No. It provides structure and prompts, but users must supply or verify current competitor facts.

faq.question: Is this useful for sales teams?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill helps explain tradeoffs, migration paths, buyer fit, and proof points for sales conversations.

faq.question: Does it recommend being aggressive against competitors?
faq.answer: No. It emphasizes accurate, fair, and useful comparisons that buyers can verify.

faq.question: Can it support programmatic SEO?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends centralized competitor data and index pages that can support larger page sets.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
